generated_comment: Honestly, in most homes this arrangement works best only. The husband handles finances, outside work, repairs and all that, and the wife manages the kitchen, children, and day-to-day running of the house—jo jahan better hai, why make it into a gender issue? These days if a man even helps a little with cooking people call him very modern, but practically speaking, women usually manage the home side more efficiently.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as merely practical and efficient rather than socially enforced. It encodes the frame through 'jo jahan better hai,' assigning men to finances/external tasks and women to domestic management, while praising minimal male domestic contribution as exceptional.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 52. A Brahmin woman who is the head of administration at a private school in Pune. You are in a staff meeting discussing the performance of a young female teacher who has been coming in late and seems emotionally distant. You know she got married six months ago and moved into a joint family. You are giving your assessment of her situation to the other staff members and what you think the school should do.
